    <h1 {...{
      "id": "panels-and-commands"
    }}>{`Panels and Commands`}</h1>
    <p>{`Learn the fundamental differences between the two types of UXP plugin components and when to use each.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "overview"
    }}>{`Overview`}</h2>
    <p>{`A UXP plugin is a self-contained application hosted in Premiere. Every plugin is built using one or both of two component types: `}<strong parentName="p">{`Panels`}</strong>{` and `}<strong parentName="p">{`Commands`}</strong>{`. Understanding the differences between these components is essential for designing plugins that fit naturally into users' workflows.`}</p>
    <p>{`Think of it this way: `}<strong parentName="p">{`Commands are actions`}</strong>{`, while `}<strong parentName="p">{`Panels are workspaces`}</strong>{`. Commands execute tasks and close; Panels provide ongoing interfaces that stay open alongside Premiere's built-in panels.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "commands"
    }}>{`Commands`}</h2>
    <p>{`Commands are `}<strong parentName="p">{`menu items that execute code when clicked`}</strong>{`. They appear in the `}<strong parentName="p">{`Window`}</strong>{` > `}<strong parentName="p">{`UXP Plugins`}</strong>{` menu and are designed for discrete tasks that don't require a persistent interface.`}</p>

    <h3 {...{
      "id": "characteristics-of-commands"
    }}>{`Characteristics of Commands`}</h3>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Execute and exit`}</strong>{`: commands run their code and finish—there's no persistent UI.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Optional modal dialogs`}</strong>{`: commands can display a `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "#modal-dialogs"
        }}>{`modal dialog`}</a>{` to gather input or show results, but the dialog blocks user interaction with Premiere until dismissed.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Lightweight`}</strong>{`: ideal for simple, focused tasks that don't need ongoing user interaction.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`No lifecycle persistence`}</strong>{`: commands don't stay "running" in the background—they execute when invoked and complete when done.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "when-to-use-commands"
    }}>{`When to Use Commands`}</h3>
    <p>{`Choose Commands when you need to:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Automate a specific task`}</strong>{`: apply a preset, export a file, or perform a calculation.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Execute a one-time action`}</strong>{`: convert clip metadata, rename sequences, or generate a report.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Provide quick utilities`}</strong>{`: show plugin information (About dialog), open documentation, or perform system checks.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Simplify the UI`}</strong>{`: when a task doesn't need ongoing interaction, a Command keeps the Premiere workspace uncluttered.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "panels"

    <p>{`To dock a panel, drag it from the title bar—not the window frame bar—until the docking overlay appears:`}</p>

    <h3 {...{
      "id": "characteristics-of-panels"
    }}>{`Characteristics of Panels`}</h3>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Persistent UI`}</strong>{`: panels stay open and accessible, maintaining their state between uses.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Non-blocking`}</strong>{`: users can interact with both the panel and Premiere simultaneously.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Dockable and flexible`}</strong>{`: panels can be docked, floated, resized, and arranged like any native Premiere panel.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Lifecycle hooks`}</strong>{`: panels have `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`create()`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`show()`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`hide()`}</inlineCode>{`, and `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`destroy()`}</inlineCode>{` hooks for managing their state.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Optional modal dialogs`}</strong>{`: like Commands, Panels can also launch modal dialogs for focused input or confirmation.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "when-to-use-panels"
    }}>{`When to Use Panels`}</h3>
    <p>{`Choose Panels when you need to:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Provide continuous interaction`}</strong>{`: display controls, settings, or visualizations that users reference throughout their session.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Offer complex workflows`}</strong>{`: present multi-step processes or rich interfaces with tabs, lists, or forms.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Integrate with workspace`}</strong>{`: give users a tool they can position and resize to fit their preferred layout.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "key-differences"
    }}>{`Key Differences`}</h2>
    <p>{`Here's a quick comparison to help you choose:`}</p>

      <thead parentName="table">

        <tr parentName="tbody">

          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Execute and complete`}</td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{

        <tr parentName="tbody">

          <td parentName="tr" {...{

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "modal-dialogs"
    }}>{`Modal Dialogs`}</h2>
    <p>{`Both Commands and Panels can display `}<strong parentName="p">{`modal dialogs`}</strong>{`—temporary windows that block interaction with Premiere until dismissed. Modal dialogs are perfect for:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Gathering input`}</strong>{`: Ask for file paths, dimensions, or configuration options.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Confirming actions`}</strong>{`: "Are you sure you want to delete?"`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Displaying results`}</strong>{`: Show completion messages, errors, or summary information.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Focused workflows`}</strong>{`: Guide users through multi-step processes that require attention.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Modal Dialogs`}</p>
    <p>{`Modal dialogs are `}<strong parentName="p">{`blocking by design`}</strong>{`—users cannot interact with Premiere until the dialog is closed. This makes them ideal for critical decisions or required input, but use them sparingly to avoid disrupting workflows.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "commands-with-modal-dialogs"

      <li parentName="ul">{`An "About" Command displays plugin information in a modal dialog.`}</li>
    </ul>

    <h3 {...{

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "combining-commands-and-panels"
    }}>{`Combining Commands and Panels`}</h2>
    <p>{`A single plugin can include `}<strong parentName="p">{`multiple Commands, multiple Panels, or both`}</strong>{`. This flexibility lets you design plugins that match user needs:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Panel + Commands`}</strong>{`: A main panel for ongoing work, plus Commands for quick utilities or batch operations.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Multiple Panels`}</strong>{`: Separate panels for different features (e.g., a control panel and a preview panel).`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Multiple Commands`}</strong>{`: A suite of related automation tasks grouped under one plugin.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`All Commands and Panels from the same plugin appear under that plugin's submenu in `}<strong parentName="p">{`Window`}</strong>{` > `}<strong parentName="p">{`UXP Plugins`}</strong>{`.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "implementation"
    }}>{`Implementation`}</h2>
    <p>{`This guide covers the conceptual differences between Panels and Commands. For implementation details, see:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><a parentName="strong" {...{
            "href": "../entrypoints/index.md"
          }}>{`Entrypoints Guide`}</a></strong>{`: Learn how to declare and implement both Panels and Commands.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><a parentName="strong" {...{
            "href": "../../tutorials/add-commands/index.md"
          }}>{`Add a Command Tutorial`}</a></strong>{`: Step-by-step instructions for creating Commands.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><a parentName="strong" {...{
            "href": "../../tutorials/add-panels/index.md"
          }}>{`Add Multiple Panels Tutorial`}</a></strong>{`: Build plugins with multiple Panel entrypoints.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><a parentName="strong" {...{
            "href": "../../tutorials/add-modal-dialogs/index.md"
          }}>{`Add Modal Dialogs Tutorial`}</a></strong>{`: Create modal dialogs for both Commands and Panels.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "summary"
    }}>{`Summary`}</h2>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Commands`}</strong>{` execute discrete tasks with optional modal dialogs—perfect for automation and one-time actions. `}<strong parentName="p">{`Panels`}</strong>{` provide persistent, dockable interfaces for ongoing interaction and complex workflows. Both can display modal dialogs for focused input or confirmation.`}</p>
    <p>{`Choose the component type that best fits your plugin's purpose, or combine both to create flexible, powerful tools for Premiere users.`}</p>

    </MDXLayout>;
}
